role: BC is the behavior policy (reference distribution) against which other policies are evaluated using OPE

methods.note: OPE evaluates target policies against the behavior policy. When the target IS the behavior policy, OPE is trivial (self-comparison).

IS.notes: Importance weights are all 1.0 when target = behavior policy

FQE.notes: BC's FQE value serves as the baseline for comparison

summary.reason: BC is the reference policy - OPE compares other policies TO BC, not BC to itself
summary.recommendation: Use BC FQE value (3.85) as baseline for comparing CQL (4.41) and DT

comparison_table.verdict: CQL shows significant improvement over BC baseline
